Petition To Determine Construction Of Trust Provisions And Instruct Co-Trustees To Perform
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Continued To Nov-02-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Be Relieved From Deemed Admissions Order Of 1-15-15
Off Calendar Apr-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gerard Launay for (moving party) Lao Chan Wong. Baron Miller for the trustees.

Mr. Miller provided oral argument regarding the tentative ruling, stating why the holding of the New Albertsons case was not applicable to this case, stated that Ms. Wong will not explain what has happened, that whether the neglect was hers or her attorneys there was neglect, that only Ms. Wong knows why she did not respond, repeatedly extended due dates and that she contributed to and created the lack of clarity. Mr. Launay stated that his client only speaks and reads Chinese, she could not read the admissions, there was an erosion of the attorney/client relationship, that she has been cooperative with him and that answers have been sent and discovery responded to. The Court asked Mr. Baron to speak in regards to any prejudice that would occur in regards to the 2nd part of the analysis. Mr. Baron stated that there would be substantial prejudice if the motion is granted, that Ms. Wong has been living in the home rent free since the death of Mr. Ho, that she rents out rooms, that the property is in a state of disrepair, that if the motion is granted she will be there indefinitely, that he received additional discovery requests today and that he will need to file a motion to compel further responses to interrogatories. The Court cited 2033.300(c) in regards to conditionally granting relief and asked Mr. Baron what would be necessary. Mr. Baron stated that they will need to produce all medical records of Mr. Ho which will be complex as they are dealing with the VAMC, they will need records from APS in San Mateo who will only produce records with a protective order, they will need an expert witness to testify to undue influence and will need to continue with discovery. The Court requests that the parties meet and confer on all outstanding discovery and determine what would have been covered by admissions, submit a further declaration by Mr. Miller regarding additional costs allowing Mr. Launay to respond. Mr. Launay stated that he has made a good faith effort to answer the interrogatories, he has cooperated in getting the personal property to the trustees, that he is new in this case and still trying to make sense of it and that his client has been cooperative to this date. Mr. Launay is to submit an amended order including the process in 2033.300(c) granting relief contingent on the parties meeting and conferring regarding discovery to put the parties back to where they were. The parties are to meet and confer no later than May 15, 2015. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on November 2, 2015 9:30 a.m., (order for the same to be sent to the parties). The Cross-Petition for return of property, etc., and the Petition to Determine construction of Trust Provisions, etc., currenty set for May 4, 2015 are to be vacated.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter: Bonnie Alphin, CSR #13798, 925-685-6222 (Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Advanced From Mar-11-15 To Dec-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Construction Of Trust Provisions And Instruct Co-Trustees To Perform
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To May-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Cross-Petition For Return Of Property, Damages, And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To May-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Baron Miller for (respondents & cross petitioners) Lolan Wong & Meilan Creger. Gerard Launay for Lao Chan Wong.

Mr. Launay reported that he has tried submitting a substitution of attorney via e-file but it was kicked back, acknowledged that discovery is owed to the trustees and will work within the court's deadline, has delivered the check for sanctions, has made it a priority to complete the remaining discovery, has arranged for the return of personal property to be done on March 12, 2015 and would like to meet with counsel to work out remaining issues. The cross-petition for return of property, etc., and the petition to determine construction of trust provisions, etc., were continued to May 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

Motion For Order Compelling Service Of Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Motion For Order Allowing Filing Of Supplement To Cross-Petition
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for Lao Wong. Baron Miller for Trustees.

Mr. Taylor referred to paragraphs 3 and 4 of Mr. Miller's declaration in support of the motion allowing filing of supplement to cross-petition and paragraphs 4 through 8 in the declaration in support of the ex parte petition to advance the hearing date, and stated that if the Court is requesting more information regarding the breakdown in relationship that he would be happy to do so in camera. Mr. Miller stated that he did not receive notice, that he is concerned it will affect the motions already on file and that if Mr. Taylor withdraws now the Client who does not speak English will not have an Attorney. Mr. Miller also stated that discovery has been conducted, that Ms. Wong is living in the home rent free and subletting rooms, that there is no discovery cutoff date and no trial date. The Court expressed concern stating that the request is being made while significant deadlines are pending. Mr. Taylor stated that he did a declaration on Friday in opposition to the Motion to Compel, that the Client appeared on Friday, that the Client said she had a new lawyer but did not provide a name, that he gave the Client all of the files, and asked how he can prepare responses when she will not work with them, cooperate with them or have faith in them. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to receipt and review of the judicial counsel form/order). The order is to reference the hearing dates of January 8, 2015 and February 23, 2015.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Ex Parte Application To Advance Hearing Date On Motion To Withdraw As Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Granted Per Written Order

Cross-Petition For Return Of Property, Damages, And Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jul-07-2014 Continued To Feb-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Lee for Lao Chan Wong. Baron Miller for Respondents and Cross-Petitioners.

Ms. Lee stated that her Client is amenable to both arbitration and mediation. Mr. Miller referred to the Trust provision regarding arbitration (paragraph 29), stated that they did not request arbitration because the issue does not involve construction or application of the Trust and requested a one week trial. Both Counsel stated that they intend to conduct discovery and agreed to a six month continuance for completion of discovery. The Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Provisions and Instruct Co-Trustees to Perform and the Cross-Petition for Return of Property, Damages and Attorney's Fees were continued to February 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and a referral to the trial department.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
